title: pavonia ellenbeckii
intro: Pavonia ellenbeckii, also known as the Egyptian hibiscus or roselle, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, characterized by its bright red flowers and edible calyces. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Pavonia ellenbeckii include reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. In herbal preparations, the calyces of the plant are often used to make tea, juice, or infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
